3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, on-stack, done, or invalid — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.happening: The root is slot 0 (or nothing if the tree is empty).
23.why: The inorder walk starts at the root.
23.invariant: root identifies where the recursion begins.

24.happening: Create a one-slot holder for the previous value (empty).
24.why: Inorder must remember the last value it emitted.
24.invariant: prev[0] carries across the whole walk.

25.happening: Walk the tree in order from the root.
25.why: A valid BST yields strictly increasing values in order.
25.invariant: answer is True only if the order never breaks.

26.happening: Return whether the inorder order held: {answer}.
26.why: Strictly increasing means a valid BST.

29.happening: An absent child — trivially in order.
A real node — traverse it in order.
29.why: Visit left, then this node, then right.
29.invariant: This node is checked after its left subtree.

30.happening: Return True for the missing child.
30.why: An empty subtree keeps the order.

31.happening: Mark node {node_id} active — its frame is on the call stack.
31.why: Amber marks the path currently being walked.

32.happening: First, walk all the way into the left subtree.
32.why: Inorder visits smaller values before this node.
32.invariant: The leftmost node is visited first.

33.happening: The left subtree already broke the order.
33.why: Once order breaks, the tree is not a BST.

33.happening: The left subtree stayed in order.
33.why: We can safely visit this node next.
33.invariant: So far values are strictly increasing.

34.why: A broken order can't be a BST.

35.happening: Now visit this node — read its value.
35.why: In order, this node comes right after its left subtree.
35.invariant: value is the next value in the inorder sequence.

36.why: We compare this value against the one before it.
36.invariant: previous is the last emitted value (or none).

37.happening: There is a previous value ({previous}) to compare.
37.why: Every value after the first must beat the one before.
37.invariant: The first node has no predecessor.

37.happening: This is the first node visited — no predecessor.

38.happening: This value ({value}) is not larger than {previous} — order broken.
38.why: A BST's inorder must strictly increase.
38.invariant: Equal or smaller means not a BST.

38.happening: This value ({value}) is larger than {previous} — order holds.
38.why: The sequence is still strictly increasing.

39.why: Its value did not exceed the previous one.
39.invariant: A red node is where the order broke.

41.why: The next visited node must exceed this value.
41.invariant: prev[0] always holds the last emitted value.

42.invariant: Visited nodes are emitted in order.

43.happening: Now walk into the right subtree.
43.why: Inorder visits larger values after this node.
43.invariant: The right subtree is checked last.

44.happening: Return whether the right subtree stayed in order.
44.why: The whole subtree is valid only if the right side is.

init.happening: Building the tree from the level-order array.
init.why: We need node objects and links before walking.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

recurse.happening: Descending left or right in the inorder walk.
recurse.why: Left is visited before the node, right after.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current path.

check.happening: Comparing this value against the previous one.
check.why: A BST's inorder sequence must strictly increase.

update.happening: Recording state or the new previous value.
update.why: Colors track the walk; prev carries the last value.

move.happening: Returning a verdict up the call stack.
move.why: A finished call hands its result to its parent.
